mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that immediately triggers equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signals supply chain normalization that supports equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during identified recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic strength that immediately boosts market sentiment, generating positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 5-day forward returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant real GDP growth creates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and force negative equity repricing over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Periods of high CPIAUCSL growth and low GDPC1 growth produce positive 60-day returns in over 60% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the unemployment rate during monetary tightening cycles indicates labor market overheating that precedes central bank intervention and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in retail sales exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate consumer demand contraction that triggers rapid equity liquidation within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances following a retail sales decline greater than 1.0 percent.
